checks.evidence: test/cli.test.js failed at the duplicate managed start assertion expecting error.code=process_already_running; the current CLI returns task_already_running. The same legacy smoke still expects logs.taskName and stop.stopped[] fields later in the flow, so the full suite is not aligned with the current US2 contract.
Failure JSON included command/status/projectRoot and runtime_state_invalid, but omitted configPath even though config resolution succeeded.

src/runtime.js can throw partial_failure for multi-task stop failures, but src/cli.js stopCommand does not catch that case to emit createPartialEnvelope, so stop partial would currently fall back to the top-level error envelope instead of status=partial with per-task results.

evidence_summary: US2 implementation evidence from T012/T013/T014 is strong on the Windows/Node local path: adapter modules exist, runtime state tests pass 5/5, managed CLI tests pass 9/9, and the earlier combined managed contract rerun passed 38/38. T015 still cannot clear because the required repository-wide validation command npm test is red, ps failure envelopes are missing configPath on a config-resolved path, and stop partial handling is not wired to the JSON partial envelope contract.
platform_evidence_level: windows_node_local_only_failed_validation; cannot claim dev-ready, platform-ready, or cross-platform-ready

residual_risks.summary: Required validation is not green because npm test fails in the existing end-to-end CLI smoke.
ps --json failure responses omit configPath for runtime_state_invalid even when config resolution succeeds, violating the CLI JSON stability rule for config-resolved commands.
stop all multi-failure paths can raise partial_failure in runtime code but are not converted into a partial JSON envelope by the CLI.
npm run check passes, but the script only syntax-checks legacy files and does not cover the new US2 tests or adapter modules, so it is weaker evidence than its name suggests.

scope_drift.decision_basis: Read scope review found no GUI/TUI/MCP/agent integration, remote/cloud, daemon, native installer, or destructive reset behavior introduced in src/adapters/process.js, src/adapters/path.js, src/runtime.js, or src/cli.js. Managed runtime remains CLI-local and recovery-oriented.

concerns: The adapter/process stop and path normalization differences are concentrated in src/adapters/process.js and src/adapters/path.js for the managed-runtime lane; the remaining process.platform branch in src/cli.js is an older doctor executable lookup path, not new US2 stop/liveness logic.
The failing repository smoke in test/cli.test.js appears to be a stale legacy baseline relative to the current US2 JSON contract, but T015 still cannot clear while that required command remains red.
One earlier combined regression had a Windows EPERM temp cleanup race after assertions passed; it did not reproduce in this T015 run because npm test stopped earlier on the stale smoke assertion.
recovery_hints: Repair or replace test/cli.test.js so the repository-wide npm test command matches the current US2 JSON contract: duplicate start should assert task_already_running, logs should assert task/logPath/content, and stop should assert process or results instead of legacy stopped[].
Wrap psCommand failures with config context so config-resolved runtime_state_invalid responses include configPath as required by the CLI JSON contract.
Teach stopCommand to emit createPartialEnvelope when stopManagedTasks reports partial_failure, preserving command/status/projectRoot/configPath and per-task results.
After those repairs, rerun npm run check and npm test on Windows and record a fresh T015 review.

checks.evidence: Includes the local lifecycle smoke, managed CLI suite, runtime-state suite, and the corrupt runtime_state_invalid config-context regression.
`psCommand` rethrows managed-runtime failures with config context, and the passing managed CLI regression asserts corrupt state payloads include `configPath` on config-resolved paths.

`stopCommand` catches `partial_failure` in JSON mode and emits `createPartialEnvelope('stop', partialStopResultsFromError(error), config)`, so stop partial paths now return a contract partial envelope with per-task `stop_failed` results.
US2 stop/liveness platform branching remains in `src/adapters/process.js`; `src/cli.js` adds no new stop/liveness OS branching and only retains the older doctor executable lookup branch.

evidence_summary: On 2026-07-07, required repository validation passed on this Windows/Node workspace: `npm run check` exited 0 and `npm test` passed 46/46. The original T015 blockers are closed: repo-wide `npm test` is green, corrupt-state `ps --json` failures now include `configPath` on config-resolved paths, and CLI stop partial failures now emit the contract partial envelope. Read-scope review found no deferred surfaces or adapter-boundary regressions in the T025/T026 repair set.
platform_evidence_level: dev-ready on Windows/Node local only; not platform-ready or cross-platform-ready

residual_risks.summary: Evidence is limited to this Windows/Node environment; no macOS or Linux lifecycle smoke was run, so broader platform claims remain unsupported.
The repaired stop partial envelope path is confirmed by current CLI source review and T026 repair evidence, but there is no dedicated repository test named for stop partial failure behavior.

scope_drift.decision_basis: No GUI/TUI/MCP/agent integration, remote/cloud, daemon, native installer, or destructive reset behavior was introduced by the T025/T026 repair window. Managed runtime remains CLI-local and evidence-preserving.

concerns: Platform evidence remains local to Windows/Node; this retry does not justify a platform-ready or cross-platform-ready claim.

`src/cli.js` still contains a pre-existing `process.platform` branch for doctor executable lookup (`where.exe` vs `which`), but no US2 stop/liveness OS branching was added outside the process adapter.
recovery_hints: If a future checkpoint needs a higher claim level, rerun the same lifecycle smoke on macOS and Linux before changing `platform_evidence_level`.
If stop partial envelope behavior becomes a recurring regression point, add a dedicated CLI test that forces one managed stop failure and asserts the `status:"partial"` stop envelope.
